title: World History
description: Broad world history prompts for daily practice in the Feed.

questions.promptText: Which event in 1914 triggered World War I?
questions.explanationText: The assassination of Archduke Franz Ferdinand in Sarajevo helped trigger the outbreak of World War I.

questions.promptText: Which treaty formally ended World War I for Germany?
questions.explanationText: The Treaty of Versailles formally ended the war for Germany and reshaped Europe.

questions.promptText: Which revolution brought the Bolsheviks to power in Russia?
questions.explanationText: The October Revolution of 1917 brought the Bolsheviks to power.

questions.promptText: What major global economic crisis began in 1929?
questions.explanationText: The Great Depression began after the 1929 financial crash and spread worldwide.

questions.promptText: Which event brought the United States into World War II?
questions.explanationText: Japan's attack on Pearl Harbor in 1941 led the United States into the war.

questions.promptText: Which organization was founded in 1945 to promote peace and cooperation?
questions.explanationText: The United Nations was founded in 1945 after World War II.

questions.promptText: What was the Cold War mainly about?
questions.explanationText: It was a long geopolitical rivalry between the United States and the Soviet Union.
options: A rivalry between the US and USSR

questions.promptText: Which wall fell in 1989 as Cold War tensions eased?
questions.explanationText: The fall of the Berlin Wall became a symbol of the Cold War's collapse in Europe.
The Soviet Union dissolved in 1991, ending the Cold War bipolar order.

questions.promptText: What term describes former colonies gaining independence after World War II?
questions.explanationText: Decolonization describes colonies becoming independent states, especially across Asia and Africa.
